title: Medicago parviflora
taxonomy: Medicago parviflora is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is a legume native to the Mediterranean region and is also known as the small-flowered medick. The classification of Medicago parviflora is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Rosids, order - Fabales, family - Fabaceae, genus - Medicago, species - M. parviflora. The plant is often used as a food source for livestock and as a green manure crop. Its classification makes it a member of the legume family, which is known for its nitrogen-fixing properties.
